title: Silent Session Capture
description: Fix session capture hook to silently skip when user has no access token or no project ID, instead of spamming warning logs on every commit. Only log errors when user is authenticated AND project is synced but upload actually fails.

title: Foundational: Reorder Capture() flow
description: WHY: Credentials check must come before project ID lookup to enable silent skip for unauthenticated users and avoid wasteful network calls.

items.item: LoadCredentials moved before GetProjectIDWithFallback
All existing tests pass

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture.go. Move auth.LoadCredentials() call from line 288 to immediately after git commit/success checks (after line 224). This makes credentials the first real check after confirming it is a git commit.
acceptance_criteria: Capture() checks credentials before project ID. No behavioral change for authenticated users with project ID.

title: US1: Silent skip when no credentials
description: WHY: Users who have not logged in should not see session capture warnings on every commit. They do not know session capture exists.

items.item: Silent return when no credentials file exists
Silent return when credentials file is malformed
Silent return when token fields are empty
Exit code remains 0

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture.go. After the reordered LoadCredentials call, return result with Captured=false and Error=nil instead of setting result.Error. This causes commands/session.go runSessionCapture to produce zero stderr output.
acceptance_criteria: No stderr output when credentials file is missing, malformed, or has empty tokens.

title: US2: Silent skip when no project ID
description: WHY: Authenticated users working on unregistered projects should not see project.id warnings on every commit.

items.item: Remove stderr warning for missing project ID
Silent return when no specledger.yaml
Silent return when project.id field missing and remote lookup fails
Exit code remains 0

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture.go. In GetProjectIDWithFallback error path, remove the 3 fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr) calls and return result with Captured=false, Error=nil instead of setting result.Error.
acceptance_criteria: No stderr output when specledger.yaml is missing or has no project.id and git remote lookup fails.

title: US3: Error logging only on real upload failures
description: WHY: Once a user has opted in (credentials + project ID), upload failures are real problems that should be surfaced for troubleshooting.

items.item: Upload failures still print warning to stderr
Metadata creation failures still print warning
Token refresh failures still queue and print
No regression in error handling

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/session.go. No changes needed - the existing handler already only prints when result.Error is non-nil. With US1/US2 changes returning nil errors, only real failures will produce output.
acceptance_criteria: Error messages appear on stderr only when authenticated user with project ID experiences upload/metadata failure. Queued sessions still show queue message.

title: US4: No regression in happy path
description: WHY: Existing session capture must continue working for users with full setup.

items.item: Successful capture prints confirmation to stderr
All existing tests pass

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture.go. The happy path (credentials + project ID + successful upload) is unchanged. Verify via existing tests and manual testing per quickstart.md.
acceptance_criteria: Session captured confirmation message still appears for successful captures. Queued message still appears for queued sessions.

title: Move LoadCredentials before GetProjectIDWithFallback
description: WHY: Credentials check is a fast local file read and must happen first to enable silent skip.

items.item: Code reordered in Capture()
go vet passes
go build succeeds

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture.go. In Capture() function, move the credentials block (lines 288-292) to immediately after the ToolSuccess check (after line 224), before GetCurrentCommitHash.
acceptance_criteria: LoadCredentials is called before GetProjectIDWithFallback in the Capture function.

title: Return silently when no credentials exist
description: WHY: Unauthenticated users must not see any session capture output.

items.item: No error set for missing credentials
No stderr output from capture function

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture.go. Replace result.Error = fmt.Errorf(not authenticated...) with a plain return result where Captured=false and Error=nil.
acceptance_criteria: Capture returns CaptureResult with Error=nil and Captured=false when credentials are missing.

title: Remove stderr warnings for missing project ID
description: WHY: Users without synced projects must not see project.id warnings.

items.item: Three stderr warning lines removed
No error set for missing project ID
result.Error remains nil

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture.go. Remove the 3 fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr) calls at lines 246-248 in the GetProjectIDWithFallback error path. Return result with Error=nil instead of setting result.Error=err.
acceptance_criteria: Capture returns CaptureResult with Error=nil and Captured=false when project ID is not found.

title: Verify upload failure error paths unchanged
description: WHY: Real upload failures must still produce visible errors for troubleshooting.

items.item: Storage upload failure still queues and reports
Metadata creation failure still queues and reports
Token refresh failure still queues and reports

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture.go and pkg/cli/commands/session.go. No code changes needed for US3 - the existing error paths for storage.Upload, metadata.Create, and token refresh failures already correctly set result.Error or queue sessions. With US1/US2 returning nil errors for expected skips, only real failures will trigger the stderr output in runSessionCapture.
acceptance_criteria: Upload failures, metadata failures, and token refresh failures still produce stderr output and queue sessions.

title: Add unit tests for silent skip behavior
description: WHY: New silent skip behavior must be verified with tests to prevent regression.

items.item: Test: silent skip when no credentials
Test: silent skip when no project ID
Test: silent skip for non-commit commands
Test: silent skip for failed commits
All tests pass with go test

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture_test.go. Add table-driven tests for Capture() covering: (1) no credentials returns silently, (2) no project ID returns silently, (3) non-git-commit returns silently, (4) failed tool returns silently. Use t.TempDir() for isolation. Tests need to set up temp git repos and control credential file presence.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify Capture returns Error=nil and Captured=false for all silent skip scenarios.
